user.title: Connect to Ashly Device
user.description: Enter the IP address of your Ashly AQM-series audio processor. Home Assistant will attempt to log in with the factory credentials (`admin` / `secret`); if those have been changed, you'll be asked for the current ones on the next screen.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the device. Look on the device's front panel under Network, or in your router's DHCP table. Do not enter a URL — just the host (e.g. `192.168.1.50`).
data_description.port: Only change if your network admin has remapped the AquaControl API to a non-standard port.

credentials.title: Sign in to {host}
credentials.description: The factory credentials didn't work on {host}:{port}, so the device must already have been hardened. Enter the current admin credentials.

data_description.username: Default `admin` on a factory device. Change this in AquaControl Portal first if your device has been hardened.

service_account.title: Create a dedicated user (recommended)
service_account.description:
Your Ashly device at {host} is still using the factory `admin` / `secret` credentials. Home Assistant can create a dedicated `{service_user}` user with only the permissions it needs (mute / preset recall / front-panel control). Your `admin` account stays untouched and you can still use it from AquaControl Portal.

If you decline, Home Assistant will run as `admin` — works, but anyone on the LAN who guesses `admin/secret` can also control your audio. You should at minimum change the admin password.

data_description.create_service_account: Recommended. Generates a random password and stores it in Home Assistant; you never need to type or remember it.

discovery_confirm.title: Ashly Device Discovered
discovery_confirm.description: An Ashly {model} was found at {host} (port {port}). Home Assistant will attempt to log in with the factory credentials when you confirm. You can override the port if your AquaControl deployment is on a non-standard one.

data_description.port: Default 8000 — only change if AquaControl was remapped.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate
reauth_confirm.description: Authentication failed for {name} at {host}:{port}. The device may have had its password changed, or the credentials in Home Assistant have drifted. Enter the current credentials below.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Ashly Device
reconfigure.description: Current connection: {current_host}:{current_port}. Update any field and Home Assistant will reconnect when you submit. Most commonly: the device's IP changed via DHCP, or you changed the password on the device.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the device.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the device at {host}:{port}. Check that the device is powered on, that you entered only the IP or hostname (not a full URL), and that port {port} is reachable from Home Assistant. The AquaControl API is on port 8000 by default — not the device's web UI port.
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Verify the username and password against the AquaControl Portal login. Factory defaults are `admin` / `secret`; the device may have been hardened in production.
error.provision_failed: Could not create the dedicated user on the device. The factory credentials were accepted but the user-creation request was rejected. Check the Home Assistant logs for details, then try again or decline the service account.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This Ashly device is already configured. To add a different Ashly device, ensure each device has a unique MAC address on the network.
abort.unique_id_mismatch: The IP address you supplied now points at a different Ashly device (different MAC) than the one this entry was originally configured against. Either correct the IP back to the original device, or remove this entry and add the new device as a fresh setup.
abort.not_ashly_device: The discovered device is not an Ashly audio processor.
abort.no_mac: The device responded but didn't include a MAC address. This typically means the device is running firmware older than 1.1.0, or the AquaControl API is in maintenance mode. Update the device's firmware via AquaControl Portal and try again.

data_description.poll_interval: How often Home Assistant polls the device for state. **60 s is the default** — push events propagate state changes in real time, so polling is mainly a drift corrector and recovery path. Lower values are only useful if the device's socket.io push channel (TCP 8001) is unreachable for an extended period. Live meter readings update at 1 Hz via a separate websocket regardless of this setting.

default_credentials.title: Ashly device is using factory-default credentials

init.title: Secure the {name} device
init.description:
The device is still using the factory `admin/secret` credentials. Choose how to harden it:

• **Provision a dedicated user** — Home Assistant creates a `{service_user}` account with only the permissions it needs, generates a random password, and stores it. Your admin account stays untouched (but you should still change its password from AquaControl Portal).

• **I've already changed the admin password** — enter the new admin credentials you set on the device.

menu_options.manual: I've already changed the admin password

provision.title: Create dedicated user for {name}
provision.description:
Home Assistant will log in to {host} as `admin/secret`, create a `{service_user}` user with only the permissions it needs, and switch its own credentials to the new account. You don't need to type a password — one will be generated and stored.

Click **Submit** to continue.

manual.title: Update credentials for {name}
manual.description: Enter the credentials you set on the device at {host}.

data_description.username: Usually still `admin` unless you also changed the username on the device.
data_description.password: The new password you set on the device.

error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Verify the username and password against the AquaControl Portal login.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the device. Check that it's powered on and reachable on the network.
error.admin_changed: The factory admin password no longer works on this device — somebody must have already changed it. Use the **I've already changed the admin password** option instead.
error.provision_failed: Could not create the dedicated user on the device. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.entry_not_found: This Home Assistant entry no longer exists.

device_unreachable.title: Ashly device has been unreachable
device_unreachable.description: Home Assistant has not been able to reach the Ashly device at {host} for several polls. The most common causes are: the device powered off, a network/cable disruption, or the device's IP address changing (e.g. via DHCP renewal). If the IP has changed, update it via **Settings → Devices & services → Ashly Audio → Configure**. This issue will clear automatically on the next successful poll.

push_stale.title: Ashly push channel quiet on {host}
push_stale.description: Home Assistant hasn't received any push events from {host} for more than 10 minutes. The integration has fallen back to polling, so state changes triggered outside Home Assistant (front-panel touchscreen, AquaControl Portal, scheduler) may be slower to appear in dashboards. The most common cause is a firewall blocking the device's socket.io port (TCP 8001). This issue will clear automatically when push resumes.

device_error.message: Operation on the Ashly device failed: {error}

preset_not_found.message: Preset {preset!r} not found on {host}. Available presets: {available}

not_ashly_device.message: Device {device_id} is not an Ashly device

entry_not_loaded.message: Ashly entry for device {device_id} is not loaded
